description_long: This object is a small, wooden biplane with a simple, handcrafted appearance. It features two parallel wings stacked vertically, supported by struts, and a single-blade propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which includes a vertical stabilizer and a horizontal tailplane. The cockpit area is open and lacks any visible controls or seating, suggesting a decorative or toy-like function. The surface is uniformly finished in a light wood tone, with visible grain patterns and a matte texture. There are no visible landing gear components, and the overall construction appears solid and minimalistic.
description: A small, handcrafted wooden biplane with stacked wings, a single-blade propeller, and an open cockpit. The surface is light wood with a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the single-blade propeller, stacked biplane wings, and open cockpit area.
description_view_1: Angled top-rear view highlighting the tapered fuselage, tail assembly, and the layered wing structure.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, open cockpit, and the streamlined fuselage shape.
description_view_3: Angled top-rear view from the opposite side, showing the tail fin, horizontal stabilizer, and wing supports.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single visible propeller at the nose; no engine details apparent.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplane.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open cockpit located forward on the fuselage, single-seat configuration, no canopy or windows.
